pattern: A trie counting how many words pass through each node lets the score of every prefix be read off as a node count, so each word's answer is the sum of counts along its insertion path.
hints: The score of a prefix is just how many words begin with it.
If you insert every word into a trie and tally how many words traverse each node, that tally is exactly that prefix's score.
Insert all words incrementing a passing count on every node, then for each word walk it again summing the counts along its path.

edge_inputs.description: single word scores its own length

stress.description: many short random words over a small alphabet to force prefix sharing
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 6)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(length)))
    return [words]
